data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with this child (S1 Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S4 30b3, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5). This child is the materialization-core slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. Empty G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a FAITHFUL snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the primary owner: its AC1 (materialize(ref) byte-matches the committed tree INCLUDING export-ignore files, faithful not git-archive) + its AC2 (LFS-pointer detection, submodule handling) deliver the faithful-snapshot half; S3 (fa49) re-roots the gates onto that snapshot. Heart of this child. Clean.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3, record+sign+in-toto) with the IMMUTABLE-SHA basis produced by THIS child (resolve ref -> immutable SHA against origin). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child: it supplies the pinned SHA but does not sign. Not an absence (S4 covers signing).
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — covered by S3 (in-place read) + S4 (signing bar). THIS child only reflects local-mode in its fetch-error path (its AC5: attested fails closed, local still runs). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the sign/read split here. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; no worktrees; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child owns the no-worktree + atomic-population + no index.lock/config.lock-contention half (its AC3 atomic all-or-nothing population + crash leaves only tmp/; its AC6 concurrent materialize of different SHAs do not contend on index.lock/config.lock; the materialize core uses GIT_INDEX_FILE temp index + dir-rename, no worktree). S2 (70c7) owns single-flight + delete-on-last-close. No double-implementation; no gap.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2 (70c7). Explicitly OUT of this child's scope ("Out: the cache/janitor (S2)"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; claims none. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered with THIS child as the primary owner: its AC5 (a private-repo fetch with missing credentials raises a descriptive, actionable error; attested mode fails closed) is the implementation; S6 (1ba5 AC2) documents the message + remedy + fail-closed/local-still-runs. Covered-by-impl + named-doc-consumer. Clean.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered with THIS child as the primary owner: its AC4 (no external tar binary; no hardcoded /tmp; runs on macOS + Linux) + its portability section (tempfile.gettempdir/mkdtemp 0700, REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR override, fcntl.flock with atomic-mkdir fallback) deliver all three sub-clauses; S6 documents REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + the EFS/NFS flock caveat (its AC5). Clean.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #1 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot of the project at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent use ...; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; unsigned local preserves verify-before-push") decomposes onto AC1-AC4/AC7. The "immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA, faithful, never the mutable checkout" + "safely under concurrent use" clauses are precisely this child's core deliverable (its AC1/AC3/AC6); the "signed/reproducible/verified_at_sha" and "unsigned local" clauses are carried by S4/S3 atop the SHA this child pins. No uncovered obligation introduced.

Note: parts of this child's "What" explicitly delegate to named consumers — descriptive credential message detail to S6 (its AC5 "see S6"), and cache/janitor + gate-wiring + signing + surfaces out to S2/S3/S4/S5. Each delegated obligation is claimed by the named sibling (S6 AC2; S2; S3; S4; S5) — covered-by-named-consumer, not absences.

Note (scope, not a G3 finding): the existing G4 review on sibling S4 (30b3) flags a seam/dependency-gap around routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's hashing path between S3 and S4. That is a consistency (G4) concern about WHO threads the handle, not a G3 union-coverage gap — the corresponding parent obligation (AC1/AC2 + ref-coherence) is still CLAIMED by children (this child pins the SHA; S4 claims claim-gate ref-coherence). G3 union-coverage of the parent criteria is complete.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the materialization-core slice (faithful snapshot at pinned SHA, atomic/no-worktree concurrency safety, portability, fetch-credential errors) of the parent criteria; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
